article:
In brief: if you're looking for a break from the heat in Abuja, you're not going to find it this week. High temperatures will be soaring into the mid-30s for much of the next seven days, with humidity making it feel even more oppressive. We do see a slight chance of rain towards the end of the week, but it's not going to be enough to bring any significant relief from the heat.

Right now, it's a very muggy morning in Abuja, with the temperature sitting at 25°C but feeling more like 31°C due to the incredibly high humidity of 94%. The wind is barely blowing at 3 km/h, which isn't doing much to help with the stifling conditions. If you're planning on being outdoors today, make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially if you're going to be out and about near the National Assembly Complex or the Abuja National Mosque.

[MAP: current satellite imagery showing cloud cover over Abuja]

Looking ahead to the rest of the day, we're expecting plenty of sunshine and clear skies, which will only serve to make the heat feel even more intense. The UV index is currently at 0, but that will change quickly as the sun rises higher in the sky, so don't forget your sunscreen if you're planning on spending time outdoors. We're expecting a high of 33°C today, which is right in line with what we've been seeing for the past few days.

### Thursday, April 10
As we head into Thursday, not much is going to change in terms of the weather. We're still looking at plenty of sunshine and clear skies, with a high of 33°C and a low of 25°C. The rain chance is currently at 0%, so you don't need to worry about carrying an umbrella if you're planning on being out and about. If you're driving into the city from the outskirts, be prepared for the usual traffic congestion around the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway and the Inner Northern Bypass.

[MAP: forecast model showing high pressure dominating the region]

One thing to keep an eye on is the heat index, which is going to make the temperature feel even more oppressive than it already is. With the humidity as high as it is, the heat index could reach as high as 38°C or more, especially in areas with lots of concrete and asphalt like the city center. This is something to be aware of, especially if you're planning on spending time outdoors or engaging in physical activity.

### Friday, April 11
As we head into Friday, the heat is going to start to ratchet up even further. We're expecting a high of 35°C, with a low of 24°C. The rain chance is still very low, at just 3%, so you don't need to worry about any precipitation. However, the increased heat is going to make it feel even more uncomfortable, especially in areas with limited shade like the Millennium Park.

[MAP: temperature forecast showing the expected high and low temperatures]

If you're planning on attending any outdoor events or activities this weekend, make sure to take the necessary precautions to stay safe in the heat. This includes staying hydrated, wearing lightweight and light-colored clothing, and taking breaks in the shade whenever possible. It's also a good idea to avoid strenuous activity during the hottest part of the day, which is usually between 11am and 3pm.

### Saturday, April 12
Saturday is looking like it's going to be more of the same, with plenty of sunshine and clear skies. We're expecting a high of 35°C, with a low of 26°C. The rain chance is still at 3%, so it's unlikely we'll see any precipitation. However, the heat is going to be intense, especially in areas with lots of people like the Wuse Market or the Abuja Arts and Culture Village.

[MAP: forecast model showing the heat index]

One thing to keep an eye on is the possibility of heat-related illnesses, especially for vulnerable populations like the elderly and young children. If you're planning on spending time outdoors, make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade whenever possible. It's also a good idea to check on your neighbors and loved ones, especially if they don't have access to air conditioning or other means of staying cool.

### Sunday, April 13
As we head into Sunday, the heat is going to start to show some signs of letting up, but only slightly. We're expecting a high of 35°C, with a low of 27°C. The rain chance is at 5%, which is still relatively low, but it's a sign that the weather pattern may be starting to shift. If you're planning on attending any outdoor events or activities, make sure to stay hydrated and take the necessary precautions to stay safe in the heat.

[MAP: satellite imagery showing cloud cover increasing over the region]

### Monday, April 14
Monday is looking like it's going to be a bit of a turning point in the weather, as we're expecting a significant increase in the rain chance. We're looking at a 35% chance of precipitation, with a high of 36°C and a low of 26°C. This is still a relatively low chance of rain, but it's a sign that the weather pattern is starting to shift and we may see some relief from the heat soon.

[MAP: forecast model showing the increased rain chance]

If you're planning on being outdoors on Monday, make sure to keep an eye on the skies and be prepared for the possibility of thunderstorms. We're not expecting any severe weather at this point, but it's always a good idea to be aware of the potential for heavy rain and strong winds.

### Tuesday, April 15
As we head into Tuesday, the rain chance is going to increase even further, to 55%. We're expecting a high of 34°C, with a low of 26°C. This is looking like it could be the coolest day of the week, relatively speaking, and it's a sign that the weather pattern is finally starting to shift.

[MAP: satellite imagery showing cloud cover and precipitation increasing over the region]

### Wednesday, April 16
Finally, as we head into Wednesday, the rain chance is going to be at 46%, with a high of 33°C and a low of 25°C. This is looking like it could be a bit of a transitional day, as the weather pattern continues to shift and we start to see some relief from the heat. However, it's still going to be warm and humid, so make sure to stay hydrated and take the necessary precautions to stay safe.

[MAP: forecast model showing the expected temperature and precipitation patterns]

Overall, it's going to be a hot and humid week in Abuja, with plenty of sunshine and clear skies. However, we are expecting some relief from the heat towards the end of the week, as the rain chance increases and the temperature starts to drop. Stay safe and stay cool, and we'll see you back here tomorrow with another update.
